title: One leapfrog step preserves a scalar discrete invariant

source.citation: Ernst Hairer, Christian Lubich, and Gerhard Wanner, Geometric numerical integration illustrated by the Stormer-Verlet method, Acta Numerica 12 (2003), pp. 399-450.
source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: For real omega, nonzero dt, current state x, and previous state xPrev, define xNext = 2 x - xPrev - dt^2 omega^2 x. Then the discrete invariant ((xNext - x) / dt)^2 + omega^2 xNext x equals ((x - xPrev) / dt)^2 + omega^2 x xPrev.
informal.latex: Let \(\omega,\Delta t,x,x_{-}\in\mathbb{R}\) with \(\Delta t\ne 0\). Define \(x_{+}=2x-x_{-}-(\Delta t)^2\omega^2x\). Then \(((x_{+}-x)/\Delta t)^2+\omega^2x_{+}x=((x-x_{-})/\Delta t)^2+\omega^2xx_{-}\).
assumptions: omega, dt, x, and xPrev are real numbers
xNext is defined by one scalar leapfrog update
informal.expected_conclusion: The discrete invariant has the same value before and after one leapfrog step.

declarations.name: leapfrogStep
declarations.statement: def leapfrogStep (omega dt x xPrev : ℝ) : ℝ := 2 * x - xPrev - dt ^ 2 * omega ^ 2 * x

declarations.name: leapfrogInvariant
declarations.statement: def leapfrogInvariant (omega dt x xPrev : ℝ) : ℝ := ((x - xPrev) / dt) ^ 2 + omega ^ 2 * x * xPrev

declarations.name: leapfrogInvariant_preserved
declarations.statement: theorem leapfrogInvariant_preserved (omega dt x xPrev : ℝ) (hdt : dt ≠ 0) : leapfrogInvariant omega dt (leapfrogStep omega dt x xPrev) x = leapfrogInvariant omega dt x xPrev

tasks.input: Translate the one-step informal invariant statement into a Lean 4 theorem statement using the provided definitions.
tasks.target: theorem leapfrogInvariant_preserved (omega dt x xPrev : ℝ) (hdt : dt ≠ 0) : leapfrogInvariant omega dt (leapfrogStep omega dt x xPrev) x = leapfrogInvariant omega dt x xPrev

tasks.input: Given the theorem statement and definitions, complete the Lean 4 proof.
tasks.target: A proof using unfold, field_simp [hdt], and ring.
